ES1! · CME · PLAN FOR JUL 16, 2026 (ETH) · BUILT THU JUL 16 ~19:30 ET

ES Trading Plan — Thu ETH

ES last 7,564.25 (−13.50 overnight) after Thursday's liquidation RTH — sellers broke price out of value mid-session to a 7,548.25 low, responsive buyers recovered it into a 7,577.75 settle bang on VAL 7,578.25, and the ETH reopen has faded it back below value on NFLX −8% after hours. Thu RTH value: VAL 7,578.25 / POC 7,586.50 / VAH ≈7,613 (line overlapped by the 7,615.00 Weak High label). Structure left behind: EOD sell-spike base 7,557.25 (spike zone 7,548.25–7,557.25, so far rejected), naked Poor Low 7,529.50 + naked POC 7,521.25 below; Wed VAH 7,619.75 and unrepaired Poor High 7,626.25 overhead, with the naked 7,646.50/7,648.75 cluster and naked POC 7,666.50 beyond. Master catalysts: US–Iran escalation (oil bid, Trump infrastructure-strike threat) + NFLX drag, into a Friday of 8:30 Housing/Imports, 9:15 IP, 10:00 UMich inflation expectations, and July opex. Must-hold: the 7,548.25–7,557.25 triple floor — spike zone fused with the 20-EMA 7,552.25 and the drawn 7,550.50 line. VIX 16.73 (+6.8%) · VX1! 18.67 · ATR 85.75.
ES last 7,564.25 · settle 7,577.75 · +12 above 20-EMA
VAL 7,578.25 · POC 7,586.50 · VAH ≈7,613
Close/last below VAL · value migrated LOWER
Floor: Spike Base 7,557.25 + 20-EMA 7,552.25 + 7,550.50 line
Overhead: VAL gate 7,578.25 · POC+Daily-P 7,586.5–7,588.75 · Weak High 7,615.00
VIX 16.73 (+6.8%) · VX1! 18.67 · ATR 85.75
NFLX −8% AH · Iran escalation · Fri = July opex
POST-CLOSE RE-ANCHOR — Thu Jul 16 RTH complete: liquidation out of value onto the 20-EMA, responsive recovery to a VAL settle, ETH now re-fading on the NFLX print Anchored to the completed Thursday Jul 16 RTH (SPX −0.51% to 7,533.77; semis −3–4% on TSM's capex raise; breadth actually positive — 8 of 11 sectors up, equal-weight +0.6%: a narrow tech-led decline, not broad risk-off). Profile read: Thursday looked above into the 7,615.00 Weak High and failed, then liquidated through POC 7,586.50 and VAL 7,578.25 to 7,548.25, leaving an EOD sell spike below 7,557.25 that was bought back — settle 7,577.75 sits exactly at VAL. Value migrated LOWER vs Wed (VAH 7,619.75 → ≈7,613); the naked Poor Low 7,529.50 / naked POC 7,521.25 below and the unrepaired Poor High 7,626.25 above are the open magnets. Master variables: the US–Iran/oil tape (escalation & oil-up = sell, de-escalation & oil-down = buy; 15-min no-trade after any major headline) and the NFLX −8% AH tech drag, resolving into Friday's 10:00 UMich inflation-expectations print and July opex. The plan: acceptance back above 7,578.25 → 7,588.75 re-enters value and targets 7,591.5 → 7,605.5 → the 7,615 weak high; failure/acceptance below 7,548.25 loses the triple floor (spike + 20-EMA + 7,550.50) and opens the 7,529.50 poor-low repair → 7,521.25 naked POC → 7,508.25. ETH = half size; counter-trend = quarter. Bias split: 40% Bear / 30% Neutral / 30% Bull — value-down + below-VAL last print + NFLX/Iran vs. an intact daily MA stack, positive breadth and a cool-CPI/PPI week. Date + catalysts reconciled via search (MOC imbalance not provided — unweighted).

01Top-Down Analysis

Daily trend → multi-day 30-min flow with the 5-min price-action recap → Thursday's completed RTH profile → VIX/macro. Reference price: ES 7,564.25 (ETH, ~19:30 ET); Thursday settlement 7,577.75.

a) Daily — fully stacked bull trend, pressing the 20-EMA dip-buy line

Daily: 20/50/100/200/300-EMA stack fully bullish · price 7,564.25 riding the 20-EMA 7,552.25 · Daily P 7,588.75 (1.552M) overhead · ATH zone 7,694 · ATR 85.75
MAValueDistance (vs 7,564.25)Status
20-EMA7,552.25−12.00 below priceHOLDING being tested — Thursday's low pierced it intraday (7,548.25), settle recovered above
50-EMA7,473.00−91.25HOLDING rising; first trend EMA below, clusters with drawn 7,469.50
100-EMA7,330.50−233.75HOLDING rising
200-EMA7,121.00−443.25HOLDING rising
300-EMA6,947.75−616.50HOLDING rising
Daily drawn levelPriceColorNote
P — pivot (1.552M)7,588.75red🔗 Sits on Thu RTH POC 7,586.50 — a POC + high-volume daily pivot cluster directly overhead
Swing High7,694.75axisDaily/contract high; drawn ATH line 7,694.00 on the 30-min drawing chart
V — value (74.085K)7,277.00blueDeep support; below the 100-EMA
P — pivot (3.223M)7,065.25redMajor volume shelf near the 200-EMA
V — value (612.994K)6,589.75blueApril-recovery base
Swing Low / P6,415.75 / ≈6,394.50redApril low; bottom P label partially obscured by a chart icon — read ≈6,394.50 (1.383M), flagged approximate
Why the 20-EMA matters here The 20-EMA (7,552.25) has been the dip-buy trigger of the entire post-April advance — every meaningful pullback since the 6,415 low has been absorbed at or just under it. Thursday drove price INTO it for the first time since the early-July dip and buyers responded within two ticks of it (low 7,548.25). A daily close below it wouldn't just be a level loss — it would be the first structural crack in the dip-buy regime, converting the 7,548–7,557 zone from demand into supply and putting the 50-EMA/7,469.50 cluster in play. Holding it keeps every decline a buy-the-dip event.
Why 7,588.75 (Daily P, 1.552M) matters 1.55M contracts of volume memory sits within 2¼ points of Thursday's POC 7,586.50. That fusion of a daily-timeframe pivot with the freshest session's fairest price makes 7,586.5–7,588.75 the single most important overhead decision: acceptance above it means the liquidation was a repricing within value and shorts are trapped; rejection there confirms supply is now layered on top of price.

Narrative. The daily remains an unambiguous bull trend — a perfectly ordered rising EMA stack, price one ATR-quarter off contract highs. But the last three daily candles show momentum stalling under 7,600–7,630 supply, and Thursday's bar is a test-and-hold of the first trend EMA. The daily says: still a dip-buy tape until 7,548/20-EMA is accepted below, with 7,588.75 the trigger that re-arms the ATH attempt.

b) 30-Min multi-day flow + 5-min price-action recap

5-min (Wed 18:00 → Thu ETH): ON high 7,632 → fade → 8:30–9:15 bounce to 7,615 → look-above-and-fail at the weak high → stair-step liquidation through 7,591.50 / 7,578.25 → 14:30–15:00 flush to 7,548.25 → responsive EOD recovery to a 7,577.75 settle → ETH re-fade to 7,564 on NFLX

5-min recap — how Thursday actually traded (open type: open-auction below value → look-above-and-fail). The overnight session inherited Wednesday's poor high: ES tagged 7,632.00 just after midnight (two ticks under the drawn 7,632.25), then bled steadily through the European morning to the high-7,570s — the first tell that Wednesday's late fade had unfinished business. The 8:30 retail-sales miss produced a counterintuitive bid (soft data = less hike risk in this regime) and ES ripped to 7,615 into the cash open. That print IS the story of the day: the rally stopped exactly at what became the 7,615.00 RTH Weak High, directly under Wednesday's VAH 7,619.75 / the 7,621.75 fib — a textbook look-above-and-fail at the value shelf. From ~10:30 the tape stair-stepped down: 7,605.50 gave way, a midday balance attempt near 7,591.50 failed, and once 7,586.50 (developing POC) and then VAL 7,578.25 broke around 13:00, the liquidation accelerated — longs from the CPI/PPI rally were carrying losses for the first time all week. The 14:30–15:00 flush printed 7,548.25, two ticks under the 20-EMA, and was immediately bought: a sharp responsive recovery reclaimed the spike zone and drove settlement back to 7,577.75, exactly at VAL. Order-flow translation: initiative sellers owned the middle of the day, but the close was responsive buyers + short covering defending the trend EMA — the day ended in an argument, not a verdict. The ETH reopen (NFLX −8% AH) has re-faded price to 7,564.25, putting that argument straight back on the table.

c) TPO Market Profile — Thursday's completed RTH

          ── ── LVN / speed zone — thin ── ──

Why the sell spike (base 7,557.25) matters An end-of-day sell spike is an unresolved auction — the market ran out of time, not sellers. Dalton's spike rules govern the next open: trading above the base (7,557.25) = the spike is rejected, sellers got nothing, lean balanced-to-higher; trading inside the spike (7,548.25–7,557.25) = the market is accepting the lower prices, caution; below the spike low (7,548.25) = acceptance of the sell-off, gap rules apply, continuation odds jump. ES at 7,564.25 is currently ABOVE the base — the constructive branch — but only 7 points above it with a negative catalyst tape.
Why the naked Poor Low 7,529.50 matters A poor low is a flat, mechanical low built by too many buy orders at one price — an unfinished auction below. Markets revisit these disproportionately often. It sits fused with the drawn 7,527.50 line, 20 points below the triple floor across a low-volume shelf: if 7,548.25 is accepted below, that LVN becomes a speed zone and 7,529.50/7,527.50 is the first destination — and simultaneously the best look-below-and-fail buy location of the whole map, because a fast repair-and-recover there is exactly how bull trends end corrections.
Why the 7,615.00 Weak High / 7,626.25 Poor High pair matters Thursday's rally died at 7,615 under Wednesday's VAH 7,619.75 — a weak, mechanical high with resting stops above it. Above that, Wednesday's own Poor High 7,626.25 is also unrepaired. Two stacked unfinished highs create a chained repair magnet: acceptance back inside value (>7,588.75) gives the auction a reason to run 7,615 → 7,619.75/7,621.75 → 7,626.25 in one sequence. They are targets, not entries — the entry is the value reclaim below them.

Narrative. Thursday printed the week's first genuine sell verdict — value migrated down for the first time since Monday and price spent the afternoon below its own POC. But the profile is not one-sidedly bearish: the close was responsive (settle AT VAL, spike bought), breadth was positive, and the destruction was concentrated in one sector. The structural map now has unfinished business on BOTH sides — 7,529.50/7,521.25 naked below, 7,615/7,626.25/7,646.5–7,648.75/7,666.50 naked above — which is exactly the geometry of a market waiting for its catalyst. Tonight's ETH decides which magnet gets fed first.

d) VIX / regime context

InputReadInterpretation
VX1! (front future)18.67 (+0.88%)BID rising off the 18.02 Wed low, but well under the 20.11 Jul-8 escalation peak and the 19.2s of Jul 13–14
Cash VIX16.73 (+6.76%)CONFIRMING vol rose WITH the selloff — no bullish divergence today; still a sub-17 absolute level
Breadth8/11 sectors up · RSP +0.6%POSITIVE equal-weight advanced — a narrow semi/mega-cap tech liquidation, not systemic de-risking
Rates/oilyields up · crude near highsHEADWIND Iran escalation keeps a geopolitical premium in energy; oil-up = equity-sell reaction function

Macro frame. The week's regime shifted from hike-fear to relief: June CPI (Tue) and PPI −0.3% (Wed) both printed cool, defusing the immediate Fed-hike scare under new Chair Warsh (next FOMC Jul 29). Thursday's damage came from micro (TSM capex → semis) and geo (US strikes on Iran continuing; Trump threatening infrastructure strikes "next week"; Iran threatening Bab el-Mandeb) — and after the bell, NFLX −8% on guidance. Friday brings 8:30 Housing Starts + Import Prices, 9:15 Industrial Production, 10:00 UMich preliminary — the inflation-expectations component is the one print that can re-ignite the hike trade — plus July monthly opex and a headline-risk weekend. VIX conclusion for the weighting: vol confirms caution but shows no panic; with breadth positive, lean bearish on structure, not on fear.

03Context Summary

The completed Thursday Jul 16 RTH, condensed to the inputs that weight tonight's probabilities.
30-min RTH+ETH: the 7,615 look-above-and-fail → liquidation to 7,548.25 → responsive settle at VAL 7,578.25 → ETH re-fade to 7,564 · naked magnets 7,666.50 above and 7,529.50 / 7,521.25 below
DimensionReadWeight for the ETH plan
Prior day typeLIQUIDATION DAY look-above-and-fail at 7,615 → PM trend down → responsive EOD recoverySellers won the day but not the close — a verdict deferred, not delivered
Settlement locationAT VAL 7,577.75 vs VAL 7,578.25 — lower third of range, but ON the value edgeClosing at the edge means the FIRST acceptance either side of 7,578.25 is the overnight signal
Profile shapeELONGATED b morning distribution 7,586–7,615, afternoon liquidation tail, EOD sell spike bought backb-shape = long liquidation, historically closer to a correction-end than a trend-start — unless the floor breaks
Value migrationLOWER Wed VAH 7,619.75 → Thu VAH ≈7,613; POC down to 7,586.50; first down-migration since MondayPrimary bear pillar
Price vs POC/VAH/VALBELOW ALL THREE last 7,564.25 = −22.25 vs POC, −14.00 vs VALES is trading below Thursday's value — the burden of proof is on buyers to reclaim 7,578.25
Extension from POCMODEST 22.25 pts ≈ 0.26 × ATRNot stretched — no strong mean-reversion tailwind either way
Daily MA contextSTACK INTACT 20>50>100>200>300 all rising; price tested and held the 20-EMAPrimary bull pillar — the dip-buy regime is intact until 7,548 is accepted below
ATR / expected moveATR 85.75 ExpMove not shown on charts — flaggedFull map 7,469–7,650 is within ±1 ATR of the settle
CatalystsHEAVY NFLX −8% AH · US–Iran escalation, oil bid · Fri 8:30/9:15/10:00 data · July opex · weekend headline riskAn unscheduled-binary night (Iran) feeding into a scheduled-print morning (UMich 10:00)
InternalsBREADTH POSITIVE 8/11 sectors up, equal-weight +0.6% on a down index dayNarrow semi-led decline — caps how bearish the broad-tape read should be
MOC imbalanceNOT PROVIDEDUnweighted in the probability defense (the responsive EOD recovery is suggestive of buy-side flow, but unverified)

04What-If Scenarios — E1–E6 (Thu ETH)

Reaction function for the night (unscheduled binary — Iran): escalation / oil-up = sell ES · de-escalation / oil-down = buy ES · 15-minute no-trade window after any major headline (the first move is routinely wrong). Scheduled morning prints: 8:30 Housing/Imports, 9:15 IP, 10:00 UMich inflation expectations — reduce risk into 10:00. ETH sizing: half size standard, quarter size counter-trend. All probabilities defended against value migration (down), VIX (bid, not panicked), MA stack (bull, intact), close location (at VAL, responsive); MOC unweighted (not provided).
E1 — Spike-Floor Hold → Rotation Back Toward ValueBULL-RESPONSIVE★★★
Zone7,548.25–7,565 entry zone → 7,578.25 / 7,586.50 targets
ContextThe EOD spike was bought once already; ES holds above the base 7,557.25 with the 20-EMA 7,552.25 and drawn 7,550.50 directly beneath. If the NFLX drag is fully priced (single-stock, non-systemic) and no Iran headline lands, the overnight auction's path of least resistance is a rotation back up to test the value edge it settled on — b-shaped liquidation days in intact bull trends resolve up from the floor more often than they trend.
SetupLONG responsive buy 7,552–7,557 on hold (or 7,565 reclaim after a higher low) · stop below 7,546 (under the spike low + buffer) · T1 7,578.25 · T2 7,586.50 · runner 7,591.50 · ~1:2.5–1:4 R:R
Confirm30-min ETH bars holding the base with buying tails; crude flat-to-lower; NQ stabilizing after the NFLX gap; VX1! fading under 18.7
InvalidateTwo consecutive 30-min closes inside the spike (<7,557.25) or any acceptance below 7,548.25 → rolls into E2
Probability★★★ 17% — the floor is genuinely strong (three fused sources) but the catalyst tape is against paying up for value overnight; this is the location trade, not the odds-on path
E2 — Floor Break → Poor-Low Repair RunBEAR★★★★
ZoneAcceptance below 7,548.25 → 7,529.50/7,527.50 → 7,521.25 · extension 7,508.25
ContextValue migrated down, ES already trades below VAL, and the overnight tape carries a fresh negative (NFLX −8%) plus a live escalation. Below 7,548.25 sits an LVN speed zone with almost no volume until the naked Poor Low 7,529.50 — unfinished business the market has a standing incentive to repair. A floor break overnight is ALSO the direction of Thursday's close-location signal, which raises continuation odds per gap rules.
SetupSHORT sell the 7,548.25 break on a failed retest of 7,552–7,557 from below · stop above 7,560 · T1 7,529.50 · T2 7,521.25 · runner 7,508.25 · ~1:2–1:3.5 R:R · half size
Confirm30-min close below 7,548.25 that holds under the 20-EMA on the retest; crude bid; NQ making new AH lows; VX1! > 19
InvalidateLook-below-and-fail — a fast reclaim of 7,552.25 (20-EMA) after a probe traps the breakdown shorts → rolls into E1's reclaim leg
Probability★★★★ 26% — highest single directional weight: three of four scored pillars lean bear (value ↓, close below value now, VIX confirming) and the catalyst mix is negative; capped below 30% because the floor is a 5-star cluster and breadth was positive
E3 — Value Reclaim → Weak-High Repair SequenceBULL★★
ZoneAcceptance above 7,586.50–7,588.75 → 7,591.50 → 7,605.50 → 7,613–7,615 → 7,619.75/7,626.25
ContextIf the overnight absorbs NFLX and reclaims the POC + Daily-P cluster, every seller from Thursday afternoon is underwater and two unrepaired highs (7,615 weak, 7,626.25 poor) sit above as chained magnets. This is the trap-reversal branch: liquidation day → full overnight reclaim → repair run. It needs an active positive (oil down / de-escalation signal / strong Asia tech reversal), not just an absence of bad news.
SetupLONG buy the 7,588.75 acceptance retest (from above) · stop below 7,578 · T1 7,605.50 · T2 7,615 · runner 7,626.25 · ~1:2–1:3.5 R:R · quarter → half on confirmation
ConfirmTwo 30-min closes above 7,588.75; crude lower; VX1! < 18.4; NQ green vs settle despite NFLX
InvalidateRejection back below 7,578.25 (back below value) → rolls into E4 balance or E2 if the floor then goes
Probability★★ 8% — a +24-point overnight reclaim against a fresh negative catalyst and an escalating geo tape is the low-odds branch tonight; it is Friday's RTH trade if the floor survives
E4 — Overnight Balance Under Value (base case)NEUTRAL★★★★
Zone7,548–7,586 rotation, centered on the 7,557–7,578 band
ContextThe market closed ON its value edge with unfinished structure both sides and THREE unresolved catalysts ahead (Iran headlines, UMich 10:00, opex flows). The rational auction behavior is to balance between the spike floor and the value gate and let Friday's prints pick the branch. Globex liquidity into an opex Friday favors rotation over initiative range extension absent a headline.
SetupFADE EDGES quarter-to-half size: buy 7,552–7,557 / sell 7,578–7,586 with 6–8 pt stops outside the band, targeting the opposite edge · take profits mechanically, no runners overnight
ConfirmOverlapping 30-min ETH bars, both-side tails, volume fading after the Asia open; crude and VX1! flat
InvalidateAny 30-min acceptance outside 7,548.25 / 7,588.75 → hands off to E2 or E3; any major headline → 15-min no-trade, then E5/E6 rules
Probability★★★★ 30% — the modal path: event-eve + opex-eve nights with an unresolved close disproportionately balance; consistent with modest POC extension (0.26×ATR) and a positive-breadth down day
E5 — Iran / Oil Headline Shock (bear tail)BEAR-EVENT★★★
ZoneGap/impulse through 7,548.25 → 7,529.50 → 7,521.25 → 7,508.25 · extensions 7,482.25 → 7,469.50–7,473 (50-EMA)
ContextTrump has publicly threatened strikes on Iranian critical infrastructure "next week" and Iran has threatened Bab el-Mandeb; the tape is one headline from an oil spike. The reaction function inverts nothing here — escalation/oil-up = sell. An impulse through the triple floor in thin Globex liquidity would run the LVN to the poor low instantly and can extend a full ATR toward the 50-EMA cluster before responsive buyers matter.
SetupRULES, NOT CHASE 15-minute no-trade after the headline · then sell the failed retest of the broken shelf (e.g. 7,548–7,552 from below) · stop above the retest high · T1 7,521.25 · T2 7,508.25 · runner 7,482.25 · quarter size only in a shock tape
ConfirmCrude gapping >2%, VX1! > 19.5, ES holding below the broken floor after the first 15 minutes
InvalidateFull retrace of the headline move within an hour (headline faded) → stand down, revert to E4
Probability★★★ 14% — elevated vs a normal night because strikes are ongoing and explicitly threatened, but still a tail: both sides have signaled they want negotiation, and the market has faded every headline this week that lacked follow-through
E6 — AH-Fade Reversal / De-escalation Pop (bull tail)BULL-EVENT★
ZoneReclaim of 7,578.25 early → gap-up toward 7,605.50 / 7,613–7,615 by the Friday open
ContextThe mirror tail: a credible de-escalation headline (talks resume, oil −2%) or a sharp NFLX-AH reversal flips the reaction function to buy. Because positioning leaned short into the close and Friday is opex, an upside squeeze through the value gate travels fast — the same thin liquidity that makes E5 violent works both ways.
SetupLONG after the 15-min window: buy the 7,578.25 reclaim-retest · stop below 7,570 · T1 7,591.50 · T2 7,605.50 · runner 7,615 · quarter size (counter-trend vs the day's flow)
ConfirmCrude −2% or better, VX1! < 18.2, NQ reclaiming its AH range despite NFLX
InvalidateRejection back below 7,578.25 → revert to E4
Probability★ 5% — requires a specific positive headline overnight; nothing scheduled can deliver it before 8:30

05Probability Matrix

Ranked for the Thu ETH session; every weight is falsifiable against the pillars listed in its defense.
RankScenarioDirectionProb★Defense (what the number rests on)
1E4 — Balance under valueNEUTRAL30%★★★★Settle AT the value edge + three unresolved catalysts ahead + opex eve = wait-and-rotate; modest 0.26×ATR POC extension gives neither side a reversion edge
2E2 — Floor break → poor-low repairBEAR26%★★★★Value migration DOWN + last print below VAL + NFLX/Iran negative catalysts + break would align with the close-location signal (continuation odds up); capped by the 5-star floor + positive breadth
3E1 — Spike-floor hold → value testBULL17%★★★The spike was already bought once; 20-EMA regime intact; but paying up overnight against this catalyst mix is the weaker half of the responsive trade
4E5 — Iran/oil shock legBEAR-EVENT14%★★★Strikes ongoing + explicit infrastructure threat = fat left tail; discounted because every non-followed-through headline this week was faded
5E3 — Value reclaim → repair runBULL8%★★Needs a +24-pt overnight reclaim against fresh negatives; the repair magnets are real but this is Friday's trade, not tonight's
6E6 — De-escalation popBULL-EVENT5%★Requires a specific positive headline; none scheduled overnight
Combined directional weightBear 40% (E2 26 + E5 14) · Neutral 30% (E4) · Bull 30% (E1 17 + E3 8 + E6 5)
Base case & reasoning — why 40 / 30 / 30 The bear edge is structural, not emotional: Thursday delivered the week's first downward value migration, ES trades below VAL, and both overnight catalysts in hand (NFLX −8% AH, Iran escalation with oil near highs) push the same way — that is worth a 10-point directional edge. It is NOT worth more, because every trend input still says bull: a fully stacked rising daily EMA ladder that just held its 20-EMA to the tick, positive breadth on the down day (8/11 sectors up, equal-weight +0.6% — a semis liquidation, not distribution), a cool CPI+PPI week that removed the immediate hike scare, and a VIX that is bid but sub-17 cash. The modal single path is E4 balance between the 7,548–7,557 floor and the 7,578–7,589 gate, with the branch decided by the next Iran headline or Friday's 10:00 UMich inflation-expectations print. To falsify the split: an overnight 30-min acceptance below 7,548.25 moves the tape to E2 and the split to ~60% bear; acceptance above 7,588.75 flips it to ~55% bull. MOC imbalance was not provided and is unweighted — the responsive settle hints at buy-side EOD flow, but unverified is unweighted.

06Decision Map

The tree roots at the ETH state and the "next headline" (unscheduled binary), then hands off to Friday's prints. Reaction function: escalation/oil-up = SELL · de-escalation/oil-down = BUY · 15-min no-trade after any major headline.
ES 7,564.25 — below VAL 7,578.25, above Spike Base 7,557.25 (NFLX −8% AH priced in the reopen)
│
├─ NO HEADLINE overnight (base path)
│   ├─ Holds 7,557.25–7,565 band → E4 BALANCE — fade 7,552–57 / 7,578–86 edges
│   │     └─ late-night lift through 7,578.25 → test 7,586.5–7,588.75 pre-open → hand E3 setup to the RTH plan
│   ├─ Sinks INSIDE the spike (7,548.25–7,557.25, 2×30-min closes) → caution — acceptance of lower prices
│   │     ├─ 7,548.25 HOLDS on the test → E1 — look-below-and-fail long back to 7,578.25
│   │     └─ 7,548.25 BREAKS + failed retest → E2 — LVN run: 7,529.50 → 7,521.25 → 7,508.25
│   │           └─ fast repair & reclaim of 7,529.50 → poor-low-repair long (best location of the map)
│   └─ Reclaims 7,578.25 and holds → value re-entry — 7,586.5/7,588.75 decides E3 vs fade
│
├─ ESCALATION HEADLINE / OIL SPIKES → 15-min NO-TRADE, then:
│   ├─ floor already gone → E5 — sell the failed retest of 7,548–52 from below · T 7,521.25 / 7,508.25 / 7,482.25
│   └─ floor held the shock → stand down — headline absorbed = underlying bid is strong (bull tell for Friday)
│
├─ DE-ESCALATION HEADLINE / OIL −2% → 15-min NO-TRADE, then:
│   └─ E6 — buy the 7,578.25 reclaim-retest · T 7,591.5 / 7,605.5 / 7,615
│
└─ FRIDAY MORNING PRINTS (hand-off to the RTH plan)
    8:30 Housing/Imports + 9:15 IP = second-tier → fade the first move unless it breaks a decider
    10:00 UMich — inflation expectations = the hike-risk print: HOT expectations = SELL, soft = BUY (regime inversion)
    Friday = July opex + weekend headline risk → reduce ETH runners into the cash open

Level-by-level: holds vs breaks

LevelIf HOLDS (as resistance/support)If BREAKS (accepted through)
7,615.00 weak highcaps third rejection of the shelf — supply confirmed, rotate back to 7,588.75 (E4/E3 fade)repair run stops above feed 7,619.75–7,621.75 → 7,626.25 poor-high repair (E3 extension)
7,586.50–7,588.75 POC + Daily Prejection shorts re-load at fair price + 1.55M pivot — back toward 7,578.25 (E4)shorts trapped Thursday's sellers underwater — 7,591.5 → 7,605.5 → 7,615 (E3)
7,578.25 VAL / settlegate shut value rejects the re-entry — liquidation read stays live, rotate down (E4 lower half)value re-entry breakdown neutralized — 80% rule risk up to the POC cluster (E1→E3 path)
7,565.00 / ES nowbid higher low over the spike base — constructive rotation toward the gate (E1)rotation drop into the pivot band — spike-base test loading (E4→E1/E2 fork)
7,557.25 spike basespike rejected sellers still own nothing below — dip-buy zone active (E1)warning trading INSIDE the spike = accepting lower prices — E2 arming, tighten all longs
7,548.25–7,552.25 triple floorDIP-BUY 20-EMA + spike low + 7,550.50 defended again — the trend's floor holds, E1 firesopens void dip-buy regime cracks — LVN speed zone to 7,529.50 (E2), close-location signal confirmed
7,529.50–7,527.50 poor lowresponsive buy repair-and-hold = correction likely complete — best long location on the map (E2 reversal leg)trend-down repair became acceptance — 7,521.25 naked POC then 7,508.25 (E2→E5 continuum)
7,521.25 naked POCvalue found old fair price attracts responsive trade — basing zoneacceleration nothing mapped until 7,508.25 → 7,482.25 (E5 territory)
7,469.50–7,473.00 50-EMAdeep dip-buy the daily trend's second line — shock absorbed, expect a violent reflexregime damage first daily-trend break since April — de-risk everything, plan resets

07Bias Flip Conditions

Starting stance for the ETH session: Neutral-Bear (40/30/30) below value, above the floor.
Starting biasFlip toTrigger levelAction
Neutral-BearBullish30-min acceptance > 7,588.75Stand down shorts; buy the acceptance retest, targets 7,605.5 → 7,615; hand E3 to the Friday RTH plan
Neutral-BearBearish accelerated30-min acceptance < 7,548.25No longs; sell failed retests of 7,548–52, targets 7,529.5 → 7,521.25 → 7,508.25; half size
Bearish acceleratedNeutralreclaim & hold 7,552.25 (20-EMA)Breakdown failed — cover, wait for the balance edges to re-form (E4)
BullishNeutralrejection back < 7,578.25Value re-entry failed — flatten longs, revert to edge-fades
Any stanceHeadline-bearescalation headline / crude +2%15-min no-trade, then E5 rules only; quarter size
Any stanceHeadline-bullde-escalation headline / crude −2%15-min no-trade, then E6 reclaim-buy rules; quarter size
All ETH positionsReduceinto Fri 10:00 ET UMichInflation-expectations print carries hike-risk inversion (hot = sell, soft = buy) — carry reduced runners only; Friday is also July opex + a headline-risk weekend

08Tactical Playbook — top 3

ETH sizing law: half size standard · quarter size counter-trend or post-headline · no fresh risk inside the 15-min headline window.
1
Triple-Floor Responsive Long (E1/E4 buy edge)
R:R ~1:2.5–1:4
▶
Entry
Bids 7,552.00–7,557.25 on a held test of the spike base / 20-EMA (buying tail on the 5-min, no acceptance inside the spike), or 7,565.00 reclaim after a higher low
Stop
7,545.75 — below the 7,548.25 spike low with a 2-pt buffer; the thesis IS the floor, so the stop lives just under it
Targets
T1 7,578.25T2 7,586.50Runner 7,591.50
Scaling
Half size on the touch, add the second half only on the 7,565 reclaim; scale ⅓ at T1, ⅓ at T2, trail the runner under 30-min lows
Kill switch
Two 30-min closes inside the spike (<7,557.25) — don't wait for the stop; the location premise is gone
2
Floor-Break Continuation Short (E2)
R:R ~1:2–1:3.5
▶
Entry
After a 30-min acceptance below 7,548.25: sell the failed retest of 7,548–7,552 from below (patience — the retest comes more often than not in Globex)
Stop
7,560.25 — above the retest structure and back inside the spike zone; if price re-enters the spike the break failed
Targets
T1 7,529.50T2 7,521.25Runner 7,508.25
Scaling
Half size total (with-catalyst but overnight); ½ off at T1 into the poor-low repair (expect a bounce THERE), rest to T2/runner only if 7,529.50 is accepted below
Kill switch
Look-below-and-fail: any 5-min reclaim of 7,552.25 post-entry — this is the E1 trap signature, exit immediately
3
Value-Reclaim Long (E3/E6, counter-trend)
R:R ~1:2–1:3.5
▶
Entry
Buy the acceptance retest of 7,578.25 from above (after 2×30-min closes over it), adding through 7,588.75; post-headline version requires the 15-min window to elapse first
Stop
7,570.00 — back below the gate means value rejected the re-entry
Targets
T1 7,591.50T2 7,605.50Runner 7,613–7,615
Scaling
Quarter size to open (counter-trend vs Thursday's flow), to half only above 7,588.75; runner off before Fri 10:00 UMich regardless
Kill switch
Rejection wick back through 7,578.25 within the first hour of entry
Position size vs ATR — the overnight math ATR 85.75 means a single ATR leg spans the entire tradeable map (7,469 → 7,650 from the settle). Every playbook stop above is 8–12 points — roughly 0.10–0.14 × ATR — which is only survivable at reduced size: half size is the ETH ceiling, quarter size counter-trend or within two hours of any headline. Friday is July opex with a hostile-headline weekend behind it: carry NOTHING full-size through the 10:00 UMich print, and plan Friday's RTH with the weekend de-risk rule in force (free-carry or flat on runners by ~14:30 ET Friday).

09Structural Warnings

The traps in tonight's map, each with its governing rule.
⚠ Sell spike, base 7,557.25 (zone 7,548.25–7,557.25) — the open-rule level The EOD spike is an unresolved auction. Spike rules for the overnight/next open: ABOVE 7,557.25 = spike rejected, constructive; INSIDE 7,548.25–7,557.25 = the market is accepting lower prices, treat every long as wrong-side; BELOW 7,548.25 = acceptance of the sell-off, gap rules apply and continuation odds jump because it aligns with Thursday's weak close-location signal. ES is 7 points above the base — one impulse from the caution branch.
⚠ LVN speed zone 7,548 → 7,529 — no-trade air pocket Almost no volume prints between the triple floor and the naked poor low. Do NOT initiate inside this void: entries live at its edges (sell the failed retest above it, buy the repair below it). Traversal is typically fast and one-directional.
⚠ Naked Poor Low 7,529.50 + naked POC 7,521.25 — repair magnets, then springboards Unfinished lows get repaired at a disproportionate rate — but repair-and-recover is ALSO how bull corrections end. Rule: the first touch of 7,529.50 is a cover/scale zone for shorts and a look-below-and-fail long candidate; only ACCEPTANCE below 7,521.25 converts it to trend continuation.
⚠ Weak High 7,615.00 / Poor High 7,626.25 — chained upside magnets, not entries Two stacked unrepaired highs mean stop fuel above 7,615. Don't short the first approach if value has been reclaimed below them — the repair sequence 7,615 → 7,619.75/7,621.75 → 7,626.25 tends to complete once it starts. Short only the failure AFTER the repair.
⚠ Extension from POC — modest, no reversion edge Last print is only 0.26×ATR below the POC: price is NOT stretched. Do not lean on mean-reversion as a thesis tonight; the auction has room to travel either way before any statistical rubber band engages.
⚠ Unscheduled binary — Iran/oil headline regime Strikes ongoing; Trump has threatened infrastructure strikes "next week"; Iran has threatened Bab el-Mandeb. Reaction function: escalation/oil-up = SELL, de-escalation/oil-down = BUY. 15-minute no-trade window after ANY major headline — the first move is routinely wrong. Half size until one clean 30-min structure confirms the branch. Reduce exposure into known risk windows (US/Israel political statements, Gulf shipping reports).
⚠ Friday = July opex + data + weekend Monthly options expiration adds pinning/gamma flows around round strikes (7,550/7,600); 10:00 UMich inflation expectations is the one print that can re-ignite the hike trade (hot = sell inversion). Weekend headline risk with an active war: Friday's RTH plan must carry the weekend de-risk rule (free-carry or flat runners by ~14:30 ET).

10Session Transition — where the Friday RTH open finds price

Gap classification is measured against Thursday's RTH range (7,548.25–7,615.00), value (7,578.25–≈7,613) and settle 7,577.75, vs ATR 85.75 (0.5×ATR ≈ 43 pts). Directional key: Thursday closed weak-in-range at the value LOW edge — a gap DOWN aligns with that signal (continuation odds up, fill odds down); a gap UP fights it (fade/fill odds up).
If Friday opens…ClassificationImplicationLikely day type
> 7,588.75Above value+POCOvernight reclaimed everything — Thursday was a one-day liquidation; repair magnets 7,615/7,626.25 activateTrend-up / repair day (E3 inherited)
7,578.25–7,588.75Inside lower valueBalance rules — no edge at the open; 80% rule risk toward the POC if acceptance builds inside valueBalanced rotation, opex pinning
7,557.25–7,578.25Outside value, inside rangeWhere ES sits now — look-below-and-fail vs. continuation is THE open decision; first 30-min IB decidesTwo-sided open, data-decided (10:00)
7,548.25–7,557.25 (inside spike)Spike acceptanceMarket accepted the sell-off overnight — sellers in control; 7,548.25 break at cash open is high-oddsTrend-down risk / open-drive lower
7,505–7,548 (gap < 0.5×ATR)Gap down, in-signalGap in the direction of the weak close = continuation favored over fill; poor low repaired overnight → watch for exhaustion-reversal at 7,521.25Gap-and-go lower OR morning reversal at the naked POC — IB decides
< 7,505 (gap > 0.5×ATR)Large gap / shockE5 played overnight — 50-EMA 7,469.5–7,473 is the reference; don't fade a headline gap without a failed lowOpen-drive / trend-down, or violent V at the 50-EMA
> 7,615 (gap up)Gap against the signalCounter-signal gap = elevated fill odds UNLESS driven by a real de-escalation headline; weak high repaired at the openLook-above-and-fail risk vs. squeeze day — headline decides

11Final Summary

DimensionCall
Macro biasBULL — fully stacked rising daily EMAs, one ATR-quarter off the ATH, cool CPI+PPI week
Intermediate biasNEUTRAL-BEAR — first downward value migration of the week; price below Thursday's value under a 7,586.5–7,588.75 supply cluster
Intraday (ETH) bias40% Bear / 30% Neutral / 30% Bull
Base caseE4 balance 7,548–7,586 into the morning, with E2 (floor break → 7,529.50 repair) the leading directional branch if the triple floor gives
Primary tradeTriple-floor responsive long 7,552–7,557.25 (stop 7,545.75, T 7,578.25 / 7,586.50) — the best-location trade of the map
Best-location alternatePoor-low repair long at 7,529.50–7,527.50 on a look-below-and-fail (only if the floor breaks first)
Real shortFailed retest of 7,548–7,552 from below AFTER a 30-min acceptance under 7,548.25 — T 7,529.50 → 7,521.25 → 7,508.25
Key bull level7,588.75 — acceptance above flips the book bullish (POC + 1.55M daily pivot reclaimed)
Key bear level7,548.25 — acceptance below cracks the dip-buy regime and opens the LVN to 7,529.50
Biggest riskAn overnight Iran escalation headline gapping ES through the triple floor in thin liquidity, straight into an opex Friday with weekend risk behind it
Day type expectationBalance-to-lower-test overnight; Friday RTH = data-decided (10:00 UMich) with opex pinning and a front-loaded de-risk rule (flat/free-carry runners ~14:30 ET)
Built Thu Jul 16, 2026 ~19:30 ET from six TradingView uploads (Daily w/ MA stack, RTH TPO profile, 30-min profile-key, 30-min drawing-key, 5-min recap, VX1!). Date + catalysts reconciled via search: SPX −0.51% to 7,533.77 Thu; semis −3–4% on TSM capex; NFLX −8% after hours; US–Iran escalation ongoing; Fri Jul 17 = Housing/Imports 8:30, IP 9:15, UMich prelim 10:00, July opex. Flags: Thu RTH VAH label overlapped by the Weak-High callout (read ≈7,613); Wed POC/VAL not labeled on the chart; Mon–Wed session closes are approximate chart/cash-derived reads; bottom Daily P label partially obscured (≈6,394.50); ExpMove not shown; MOC imbalance not provided — unweighted. ETH sizing: half standard, quarter counter-trend. Not financial advice — a structured read of the charts provided.
